para.bold: Pointer to the data for the time step (tstp). 'data_+ $ t$ * element_size' correponds to (0,0)-component of $ G^R(tstp,t)$, 'data_+ $(tstp+1+\tau)$ * element_size' correponds to (0,0)-component of $ G^\rceil(tstp,\tau)$, 'data_+ $(tstp+1+n_{\tau}+1+t)$ * element_size' correponds to (0,0)-component of $ G^<(t,tstp)$.
Size of the Matrix form; size1*size2.
Number of the time grids on the Matsubara axis.
Number of the colums in the Matrix form.
Number of the rows in the Matrix form.
Size of the data stored for the time step; $ (2*(tstp+1)+(n_{\tau}+1)) $ \;* element_size

memberdef.argsstring: (int tstp, int ntau, int size1, int sig)
memberdef.name: herm_matrix_timestep

memberdef.argsstring: (int tstp, int ntau, int size1, int size2, int sig)
memberdef.name: herm_matrix_timestep

memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

memberdef.argsstring: (int tstp, std::vector< int > &i1, std::vector< int > &i2, herm_matrix< T > &g, std::vector< int > &j1, std::vector< int > &j2)
memberdef.name: set_submatrix

memberdef.argsstring: (int tstp, std::vector< int > &i1, std::vector< int > &i2, herm_matrix_timestep< T > &g, std::vector< int > &j1, std::vector< int > &j2)
memberdef.name: set_submatrix

memberdef.argsstring: (int tstp, int ntau, int size1, int root)
memberdef.name: Bcast_timestep

memberdef.argsstring: (int tstp, int ntau, int size1, int root, int tag)
memberdef.name: Recv_timestep

memberdef.argsstring: (int tstp, int ntau, int size1, int dest, int tag)
memberdef.name: Send_timestep

para.bold: Class herm_matrix_timestep deals with contour objects $ C(t,t') $ at a particular timestep $t'$.

detaileddescription.para:
Purpose
The class 'herm_matrix_timestep' describes a time slice $\mathcal{T}[C]_n$ of the contour function $C(t,t')$ at the time step $ n $, storing $C^<(t_j, t_n), C^\mathrm{R}(t_n, t_j)$ for $j=0,\dots,n$ and $C^\mathrm{M}(\tau_m), C^\rceil(t_n, \tau_m)$ for $m=0,\dots,N_\tau$. The contour function $ C(t,t') $ can be of scalar type or matrix-valued.
